goal: Guide an LLM to rewrite short-drama dialogue into natural regional spoken language before constructing a talking-video prompt.
decision_order: Identify dramatic intent, relationship, emotional pressure, and scene formality.
Use a stored line when the intent matches.
For a new line, imitate the profile's lexical_patterns and existing examples.
Prefer light localization for leads and emotional scenes.
Use strong localization only when requested or when the role is regionally distinctive.
Return one spoken line plus a short delivery note.
Check that the line remains understandable and speakable within a 10-second clip.

hong_kong_code_switching.light_rule: Use natural Cantonese first. English insertion is optional and usually zero or one common spoken term.
hong_kong_code_switching.strong_rule: Use one or two natural English insertions at most. Keep Cantonese grammar as the base.
avoid: Do not translate every noun or verb into English.
Do not use English merely to sound fashionable.
Do not mix formal business English into intimate dialogue unless the character would naturally do so.
Do not exceed two English insertions in a short 10-second line.

required_output.assumptions: Only include when the user omitted important context.
